title: Fantasy Side Quest Hooks
description: Brief, immediately usable side quest seeds for fantasy settings — dropped into a session as overheard rumors, posted notices, or NPC requests.

entries.result: A shepherd near the village is missing three sheep and is too frightened to say what took them. The tracks don't match anything local.
The miller's well has gone strange — the water comes up warm and smells faintly of metal. The miller can't afford a mage.
A locked chest washed up on the riverbank three days ago. The town hasn't opened it because no one can agree on what to do when they do.
A woman at the market claims her face has been stolen — replaced by another, subtly wrong. No one else can see the difference.
A bridge on the road north is down. Someone claims it was sabotage. Someone else claims the river did it. The road is closed and trade is suffering.
A monk from a distant monastery is looking for a relic stolen from their order decades ago. They believe it's somewhere in this town.
Children have started disappearing at dusk. They return by morning with no memory of where they've been and soil on their shoes.
A retired soldier needs someone to deliver a letter to their former commander before they die. The letter can't be trusted to the post.
An estate on the outskirts of town has been abandoned for twenty years. The new owner has hired people to clear it out. The clearing-out crew hasn't come back.
A traveling merchant was robbed on the road and is offering double the stolen goods' value to recover them. The robbers haven't gone far.
The local temple's donation box has been emptied three nights running. A sleeping spell was used. Someone with training is involved.
A ghost has started appearing in the market square at noon — fully visible in daylight — and it looks exactly like the town elder.
A scholar is paying for unusual plants from the forest. One of them has been found, and it wasn't growing there last month.
The town's new watchman has been asking too many questions about old families. Someone wants to know who they're working for.
A farmer found a trapdoor in their field that wasn't there during spring planting. It's locked from the inside.
A local noble is hosting a tournament. The prize is significant. The noble's champion has never lost in suspicious ways.
Someone in town is sending ravens to an unknown destination. The messages are written in a cipher. The someone doesn't know the ravens are being intercepted.
A dwarven artisan claims the sword they finished last week was switched out while it dried — replaced with an identical but inferior blade.
A traveling family is sheltering at the inn with a child who speaks a language no one in town recognizes. The family won't say where they came from.
An old herbalist has stopped selling remedies and locked their shop. Neighbors hear movement inside. No one answers the door.
A cart driver found a body on the road and brought it to town. The body has no wounds, no belongings, and no one recognizes the face.
A fisherman caught something in the river that he refuses to describe. He sold everything he owned that afternoon and is trying to leave town.
A shrine on the edge of the forest has been destroyed. The local temple considers this a provocation. The forest considers it the same.
Someone is paying above market rate for old iron — specifically old iron, from before the current foundry. No one knows why and the buyer won't say.
A letter arrived at the inn addressed to a dead man. The innkeeper held it. It has since begun to smell of something and feels warmer than paper should.
The town's graveyard has three fresh graves that no one can account for — no death records, no mourners, no names on the markers.
A young apprentice needs a rare ingredient found only at a dangerous location. Their master has forbidden them from going. The master hasn't said why.
A wealthy widow is offering a reward for information on who poisoned her prized garden. The garden is in ruins. The husband died last spring under similar circumstances.
A gang has been collecting 'protection' from market stalls. The watch is aware. The watch is being paid more than the stall holders.
A cartographer is mapping the old road and wants a guard for one section near an abandoned waystation. They'll pay well and won't explain the extra rate.
A mage's tower in the hills went dark three nights ago. No smoke, no light, no response to signals. The mage had no apprentices.
A thief has been stealing things of no value — old shoes, broken pottery, river stones. No one is worried. One person is very worried.
The road to the capital is rumored to be watched by something. Three merchants have arrived describing the same experience at the same mile marker.
A mine has been closed after the workers reported sounds from below the deepest shaft. The sounds started after they broke through a new vein.
An artist was commissioned to paint a portrait of the town's founding family. While working from old records, they found a face that appears in the records that no one in the family can account for.
A halfling child has gone missing. The parents are searching. The other children say she went to talk to the woman in the well.
A merchant guild claims a competitor has been diluting their goods. The competitor claims the same. A neutral party is needed to determine which accusation is real.
A traveling circus is passing through. Three of their performers are looking for work that doesn't involve the circus.
The local constable was found this morning locked in the jail from the inside. They don't remember the night before.

A woodcutter is offering half their land deed to anyone who recovers their grandmother's locket from where she dropped it in the river. The grandmother was a hedge-witch. The river runs through old territory.
A series of strange fires have broken out in the warehouse district. No accelerant, no witnesses, and the fires burn a color that fire shouldn't.
A noble family needs an heir delivered safely from one city to another. The heir is an infant. Three previous couriers have turned down the job.
An old treaty requires a town to deliver tribute to a forest village that may or may not still exist. The tribute hasn't been paid in forty years and something has come looking for it.
A guard post on the mountain road hasn't reported in two weeks. The army doesn't want to send soldiers until they know what they're sending them to.
A smuggler has hidden a shipment inside the town somewhere and needs help retrieving it without being seen by the people who are also looking for it.
A plague of insects has descended on one specific farm. Not the farm next to it — one farm. The farmer wants to know why.
An old man is paying for stories — not performing them, just collecting. He's particularly interested in stories about a specific road, a specific time of year, and a specific direction of travel.
A noble is looking for a discreet person to investigate whether their butler is stealing. The butler may be blackmailing the noble. The noble may be framing the butler.
A shipment of medicine destined for a quarantined village has been stolen. The village has two weeks.
An old soldier has a trophy from a famous campaign — a sealed box taken from a defeated enemy. They never opened it. They're dying now and want someone else to decide what to do with it.
A river has changed course slightly since last season. The change has flooded a crypt. The crypt belongs to a family that doesn't want it investigated.
Someone is distributing pamphlets in the market that contain accurate information about local officials' corruption. The officials want to know who. The public wants more pamphlets.
A strange fruit has been sold at the market for three weeks by an unfamiliar vendor. People who eat it report vivid shared dreams. The vendor hasn't been seen in two days.
A young woman claims to be the prophesied one from a local legend. The prophecy is vague enough that it could be her. The local cult thinks so too.
A merchant is trying to recover a debt from someone in the next town who claims the debt was paid. Someone is wrong, or one of them is lying.
A beloved local dog has died and its owner is convinced it was poisoned. The owner has considerable political influence and wants answers.
An old map found in a new building's foundations shows a building that was supposedly destroyed a century ago — intact, and in a location that is currently underwater.
A farmer's crop is growing at double speed. It's already twice the expected yield. They're afraid to harvest it because it's still growing.
A toll bridge is being extorted by someone upstream who has dammed the river and is offering to release the water for a fee.
A newly arrived family has bought a house that the previous owners swore was uninhabitable. The new family seems fine. Their neighbors are not fine.
A priest has been missing from their temple for three days. Their personal effects are still there. Their vows would prohibit leaving willingly.
A wandering herbalist is selling remedies that don't work. Several people have gotten sick. Finding the herbalist before they leave town is time-sensitive.
An animal — a deer, a boar, a heron — is following a specific person through town. It won't be chased off. It's been doing this for a week.
A ship arrived at the docks this morning with the harbor entry log correctly filled, the manifest in order, and no crew.
A famous sword has been found in a pawnshop. The sword belongs to someone who died in a war two kingdoms away. Someone needs to find out how it got here.
A local criminal is trying to go straight and keeps being pulled back in. They want help arranging a permanent exit from their former associates.
A child drew a detailed map of a place they've never been. The location exists. No one has explained how the child knew what it looked like.
A wealthy patron is willing to pay for a dangerous escort mission but won't say where. They'll reveal the destination on the road.
A secret society is recruiting. The invitation was left for the wrong person. That person wants to know who it was meant for.
A beggar in the market has been handing out coins — not receiving them. The coins are old, very old, and one side of each is blank.
A bounty hunter has arrived in town looking for someone. The description matches several people. The person being hunted may or may not have done what they're accused of.
A builder's crew broke through a wall in an old building and found a sealed room containing furniture, food, and a fire that was still burning.
A wizard's tower is for sale at a significant discount. The selling agent is vague about why the previous occupant vacated.
A noble has bet their estate on the outcome of a horse race. They want someone to ensure the result. The other bettor has the same idea.
An old hermit on the hill has started predicting minor local events with perfect accuracy. They claim to know something large is coming. They're not specific.
A series of thefts have occurred only on nights with no moon. Nothing valuable is taken — only sentimental items.
A foreign dignitary's horse went lame outside of town. The dignitary is stranded and their escort is growing impatient.
An orchard has begun to bear fruit out of season — the wrong fruit, on the wrong trees, in mid-winter.
A message was found in a bottle — not from a shipwreck, since the nearest sea is four hundred miles away.
A village upstream has stopped sending its weekly market goods. A runner sent to check on them hasn't returned.
A traveling preacher has attracted a large following in a very short time. Their sermons are oddly specific about what listeners should give up.
A cat that belongs to a merchant's household has been found dead, reanimated, dead again, and is now missing.
A noble family is feuding over an inheritance and both sides want a neutral party to find the original will, which has disappeared.
A local legend says that every hundred years, something leaves the forest and walks into town. The interval has elapsed.
A river barge arrived with its captain dead, its cargo intact, and its logbook locked in the captain's cabin with the last entry reading: 'Do not open.'
A prisoner being transported to the capital has escaped. The escort is asking for local help. The prisoner has a different account of why they're being transported.
A new road is being built through territory that someone claims is sacred. The builder disagrees. Arbitration has failed. Someone is about to do something rash.
A local alchemist has produced something they can't safely dispose of and needs help moving it to a location where it can be neutralized.
A legendary beast is rumored to be nesting near the trade road. It hasn't attacked anyone yet. Three separate parties have gone to investigate. All three are missing.
An elderly nun has left her convent for the first time in fifty years and is looking for a person she wronged. She has a week, maybe less.
A duel is scheduled between two merchants. One of them hired a professional. The other one didn't know dueling was an option until yesterday and is very frightened.
A magic item was donated to a temple charity auction. It was never supposed to be donated — the original owner wants it back without causing a scene.
A famous old tree in the center of a village has died overnight. The roots go down deep, past where roots should reach, and something is on them.
A census taker is three weeks overdue from a routine count of a remote settlement. The settlement is known to be unfriendly to outsiders.
A child has been having the same nightmare every night for a month and has begun drawing it. The drawing matches a location that exists.
Two rival inns are at war, escalating from bad reviews to property damage. Both owners want outside mediation. Or outside muscle. Preferably both.
A wandering seer sold several people in town prophecies that all referenced the same date. That date is approaching and none of them want to be alone when it arrives.
An old soldier died and left their home to 'the first honest person to knock.' Several people have knocked. Something is evaluating the honesty of each one.
A musician in the market plays a tune that the party recognizes — though they've never heard it before and wouldn't know why they recognize it.
A festival is approaching, and someone has stolen the ceremonial object without which the festival cannot occur. The town believes the year will go badly without it. They may be right.
A local magistrate is being blackmailed. They want the blackmailer found. They haven't told anyone what the blackmail is about.
A river ferry has been running without a ferryman for three weeks. It crosses at regular intervals. No one has tried to board it.
A wanted poster has been placed in the town square for a crime that no one in town has reported.
A street beggar is systematically avoiding a specific block of town. They won't say why, but they'll physically resist being led there.
A woman is looking for the man who promised to marry her. She has his name, a physical description, and a contract witnessed by two people who are now unavailable.
An herbalist's supply of a rare ingredient has been taken — not stolen, but methodically transplanted. Someone is trying to move the supply, not just take it.
A temple bell rang at midnight for the third night running. The bell has no clapper.
A local family claims their patriarch has been replaced by something wearing his face. The patriarch denies this vigorously.
A visiting merchant keeps asking about old graves — not the cemetery, but the unmarked ones outside of town.
A wealthy landowner offers a generous reward for anyone who can clear out their root cellar. They won't describe what's in it. They won't open the cellar door themselves.
A clockwork device was found in the woods. It does something. No one knows what or who built it.
The village idiot — beloved, harmless, a fixture for twenty years — suddenly began speaking in coherent, urgent, untranslatable sentences three days ago.
A map seller at the market has a map of a dungeon for sale. It appears to be recently made and in good condition. The dungeon it maps is supposedly unexplored.
A local guard found a tunnel under the market square while investigating a sinkhole. The tunnel goes further than it should.
A retired adventurer is giving away their equipment. They say they don't need it anymore. They look well. They seem at peace. This is somehow the most alarming thing about them.
A wedding has been disrupted by the reappearance of a person who was supposed to be dead — specifically, the betrothed's first spouse.
A local scholar believes they've identified the location of a legendary library. They need someone to go look, because they're too old and too careful to go themselves.
A missing child has been found, but refuses to return home and won't say why. The parents are insisting. The child is insisting harder.
A guild is hiring guards for an unusual shipment: not valuable, not dangerous, but requiring extreme discretion. They haven't specified what 'discretion' means.
Three unrelated people in town have started sharing the same dream. They don't know each other. They figured it out when each of them described it to a local priest.
A messenger bird arrived with a message that wasn't intended for anyone in town. The message is half burned and describes something that shouldn't exist.
The town's oldest resident, who has lived through three generations, recently started asking who built the old well. Specifically, who really built it.
Something has been howling outside the village on windless nights. The sound is coming from a direction with nothing in it.
A blind herbalist claims she can smell magic on a specific building in town. The building's owner is very unhappy about this claim.
A series of payments has been found, written in the ledger of a dead merchant, to someone who has no identity in town records.
A soldier returning from war is trying to get home but their hometown no longer exists on any map produced in the last year.
A local crime boss wants a specific item retrieved from a location they own but cannot access without causing a public incident.
A series of animals have been found arranged in a deliberate pattern outside the town, on a road leading somewhere specific.
A woman is looking for her brother, who left for the mines two months ago. The mines are still operating. Her brother is not there.
A letter of credit was forged using a seal that only three people have access to. Two of them have perfect alibis. The third is the forgery victim.
A festival candle that was supposed to burn for three days went out at midnight on the first. The tradition says this has only happened twice before, both preceding disasters.
A local pickpocket was caught with a locket that hasn't been made in this region for two hundred years. The pickpocket doesn't know where they got it.
A merchant ship is docked past its scheduled departure and the captain won't explain why. The crew is getting nervous.
A traveling healer came through and cured a disease that the local physicians said was incurable. They left before anyone could ask how.
An annual tradition requires the town to leave an offering at an old cairn on the hill. This year the offering was accepted and something was left in return.
A tax assessment error has inadvertently given a poor family rights to land a wealthy family has occupied for thirty years. Both have legal standing. Both are right.
A house caught fire. The fire went out on its own. The house is intact. The family inside has no memory of going to bed the night before.
A young noble has run away to become an adventurer. The family wants them found and returned. The noble has skills the family doesn't know about.
A local business has been purchased by an outside buyer who has made no changes, kept all the staff, and visited once. No one knows what they actually bought.
A bridge toll has been collected for a hundred years by the same family. The latest collector has not been seen in ten days and the toll hasn't stopped being collected.
A newly constructed building keeps settling in ways the builder can't account for. Something under it is moving.
A wandering scholar is willing to pay handsomely for a specific type of stone found only in a specific location that is also home to something unfriendly.
Three witnesses claim to have seen the same thing at the crossroads at midnight. Their accounts agree in every detail except one, and the difference matters.
A curse has been placed on a family home. They know it's a curse. They know roughly when it was placed. They don't know what it does.
A refugee from a distant land has arrived with valuable skills, credible claims of persecution, and something wrapped tightly in cloth that they won't let out of sight.
A local fortune teller — previously believed to be a fraud — told a merchant their ship would sink. The ship sank. The fortune teller is now in very high demand.
A road crew repairing a stretch of highway uncovered a stretch of older road underneath — in perfect condition, running in a slightly different direction.
A local criminal organization has been paying protection money to something. The payments have stopped. The organization is frightened.
A town official is selling building permits for land they don't own. The land is on the edge of the forest and no one has explained why the official thinks it can be sold.
An old shrine has been vandalized. The locals are upset. The vandals left something at the shrine instead of taking anything.
A cartographer's assistant found a map inside an old map — a second image visible only at a certain angle of light.
A merchant died and left their fortune to charity. The charity doesn't exist. Someone is setting it up in real time to claim the money.
A traveling circus performer has information that powerful people want. They'll trade it for safe passage to the next city.
Something is messing with the market weights. Not enough to be obvious — just enough to consistently favor a specific vendor.
A local militia has been quietly training in secret. The town leadership doesn't know. The question is whether this is protective or preparatory.
An old letter surfaced describing a hidden room in a building that is now a functioning inn. The innkeeper doesn't know about the room.
A town well went dry. Divination says water is twenty feet lower. Something is consuming it from underground.
A hermit who lived in the hills for twenty years has come down and is asking for someone to accompany them back up. They won't go alone.
A merchant's warehouse was broken into. Nothing was stolen. Notes were left on each item indicating exactly how much they were worth. The amounts are correct.
An old map shows a road through a region that is currently a marsh. Records suggest the marsh formed within living memory. Whatever happened, someone knows.
A letter intended for someone else was delivered to a local family and contains instructions that have already been followed by someone.
A ghost seen haunting a crossroads looks like it's waiting for someone. Anyone who stops to talk is given a message that makes no sense.
An abandoned mine that was supposed to be played out has been producing small amounts of ore again — just enough to attract new workers and to be suspicious.
A noble family has hired a food taster for the first time in memory. They won't say why.
A traveling party of holy warriors is passing through and insisting on searching homes for something they describe only as 'the unclean thing.' They have legal backing from a distant authority.
A local drunk swears they saw something come out of the river and walk into the city on two legs and not come back out. They are, unusually, completely sober when reporting this.
A strange game has spread through the children of town — specific rules, specific words, specific gestures. No one knows who started it. The rules are old.
A door in town has appeared that was not there yesterday. It is locked. Light comes from under it. The building it belongs to doesn't have a room on the other side.
The road to a neighboring village is clear, but travelers keep arriving from it several hours later than expected, with no explanation for the time.
A locked box in the mayor's office has begun to make a sound. It has been locked for fifteen years. No one still working there has the key.
A merchant is offering good money for something specific recovered from a creature's lair. They won't say why they want it. The creatures aren't the problem — the reason is.
An old soldier who fought in a war forty years ago claims to have seen one of the enemy commanders alive in the market yesterday.
A fire destroyed a building and the owner is claiming it was arson. The city watch says it was an accident. The owner has reason to believe both parties are wrong.
A river is running a different color than usual for the third day. Upstream, no one's reported anything. Downstream, animals won't drink from it.
A local beggar has been giving people small, specific pieces of advice that turn out to be correct. They accept only one type of coin as payment and have never spent any of it.
A new house of worship has appeared overnight — fully built, lit, with services already underway. No one saw it being constructed.
A traveling knight has arrived looking for a specific person to challenge to a duel. The person they're looking for moved away years ago. The knight doesn't believe it.
A lockbox at the banking house contains something that two different people claim to have deposited. Both have paperwork.
A night-blooming plant that only flowers once every ten years has bloomed early. The last time it bloomed early, the records from that year are redacted.
A newly elected official made a promise on the campaign trail that someone is now insisting they fulfill — in a way the official never intended.
A cat in town has been bringing the same person gifts of increasing strangeness: first a mouse, then a key, then a tooth.
A local crime boss was found dead with no marks on them. Three other crime bosses in neighboring cities died the same way in the same week.
A letter from a dead person arrived at the post three days after the sender died. The postmaster swears it came in with the regular deliveries.
A woman is selling maps she says she drew from her dreams. The locations on the maps are real. The things in those locations that she's drawn haven't been seen there yet.
A disputed plot of land is being claimed by three parties simultaneously. One of them is a person who has been dead for ten years, and whoever is filing on their behalf is very committed.
The oldest building in town is scheduled for demolition. A local historian is convinced something is hidden in the walls. The owner wants it down by end of week.
A set of bones was found in a foundation. The bones are old. They were placed deliberately. And there are too many for one person.
A jar of an unknown substance was left on the temple steps with a note that says only 'for the time when you need it.' No one knows who left it.
An island in the river that has always been empty has a light on it tonight. And last night. And the night before.
A blacksmith's apprentice made something without knowing what it was for and has since realized it's a component of something larger. They want to find out what.
Someone has been leaving food out for something at the edge of town. The food is always gone by morning. Whoever is leaving it is doing so without the town's knowledge.
A famous hero who retired in this town has left town with no notice, no explanation, and their weapons still on the wall.
A woman has arrived claiming to be the twin sister of a woman who died here last year. No one knew there was a twin.
A merchant is selling passage through a shortcut in the forest that has shaved three days off travel time. Other travelers are arriving three days early with no memory of how.
A rash of very minor pranks — objects rearranged, messages altered, doors opened — has everyone pointing at everyone else. The pranks are too coordinated to be multiple people.
A traveling theater company is performing a play that describes events that happened in this town but before the company ever visited it.
A minor noble has gone quiet after receiving a letter with a specific seal. Three days later they're acting normal but taking a different route to work.
A dog has been sitting in front of a specific door in town for six days, refusing to be moved, refusing to eat, making no sound.
An old festival tradition requires the lighting of a beacon fire that hasn't been lit in forty years. This year, the beacon lit itself.
A stranger rode into town, paid for a week's lodging in advance, asked one question about a specific road, and has not left their room since.
